title: 10 best herbal creams for insect bites

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling, redness, and itching caused by insect bites, providing quick relief to affected areas.

Antiseptic: The antiseptic properties of pot marigold cream prevent infection and promote healing by killing bacteria and other microorganisms that may enter the bite wound, reducing the risk of secondary infections.
Antihistamine: Pot marigold's antihistamine property helps alleviate itching, irritation, and allergic reactions caused by insect bites, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds present in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, work together to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with insect bites. Additionally, its antiseptic properties help to prevent infection and promote healing, making it an effective remedy to alleviate discomfort and promote quick recovery from insect bites.
remedy_constituents: N-ethylcarboxamide pyrrolidone (NECP): Helps with insect bites by acting as a potent anti-inflammatory agent, reducing redness and swelling.
Calendulin: Assists with insect bites by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, preventing infection and promoting wound healing.
Calendol (triterpene saponins): Aids in the relief of insect bites by displaying anti-inflammatory and antipruritic (anti-itch) effects, reducing discomfort and itching sensations.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and protect the skin from insect bites.
Leaves: They are often used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and promoting healing.
Seeds: They are sometimes used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce redness and itching associated with insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers by cutting the stems at an angle to promote re-growth and collect 1 cup of fresh flowers in a clean basket.
Dry the calendula flowers by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper towel for 24 hours.
Infuse the dried calendula in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil by mixing in a clean glass jar for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused calendula oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids to get 1 cup of calendula oil.
Mix 1/2 cup of calendula oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, reduce swelling and redness caused by insect bites, while its antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate the skin response.
Soothing and calming effects: The gel of the aloe plant contains polysaccharides and glycoproteins that calm the skin, reducing itching, pruritus, and discomfort associated with insect bites.

Antimicrobial properties: Aloe vera's antimicrobial compounds, such as salicylic acid and cinnamic acid, help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can occur due to insect bites, preventing secondary infections and promoting healing.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant reduces redness and swelling caused by insect bites, making it an effective remedy for itchy and irritated skin. Aloe vera's natural cooling sensation calms the skin, providing quick relief from the discomfort and pain associated with insect bites. Its antibacterial properties also help prevent infection and promote faster healing.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the redness and swelling caused by insect bites, providing relief and promoting healing.

Catechin: As a phenolic compound, catechin has potent antioxidant properties, which help neutralize the histamine and other inflammatory substances released during an allergic reaction to insect bites, thereby reducing itching and discomfort.

Allo-iso-Aloe-emodin: It has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and protect the affected area from infection and promote wound healing, reducing the risk of complications from insect bites.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their gel is rich in anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making it an effective ingredient in creams for reducing itching and swelling caused by insect bites.

Gel (from the Leaves): This clear, jelly-like substance is extracted from the leaves and is known for its cooling and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to alleviate discomfort and irritation from insect bites.

Aloe vera Extract (from the Leaves): This extract is made by dissolving the gel in a solvent and is often used as an active ingredient in creams and ointments to provide relief from insect bites due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize your equipment and workspace to prevent contamination and ensure a safe process.
Combine 100g of aloe vera gel with 50g of distilled water in a mixing bowl.
Add 5g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil to the mixture and heat until melted.
Stir in 5g of vitamin e oil and 2g of preservative powder to enhance shelf life.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and thicken for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild indigo contains compounds that reduce inflammation, swelling, and redness associated with insect bites, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antiseptic and antimicrobial properties: The cream's antiseptic and antimicrobial properties help to prevent infection by killing bacteria and other microorganisms that may enter the bite wound, reducing the risk of complications like abscesses or cellulitis.
Soothing and calming effects: Wild indigo has been traditionally used for its ability to calm irritated skin and reduce itching associated with insect bites, providing a sense of comfort and relief.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Baptisia tinctoria, also known as wild indigo, creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream reduces swelling and redness associated with insect bites, providing relief from discomfort and itching. The antiseptic properties also help to prevent infection, promoting a faster recovery. Additionally, Baptisia tinctoria is rich in antioxidants, which help to neutralize free radicals and soothe the skin, making it an effective natural remedy for insect bites. Its cooling effect also provides instant relief.
remedy_constituents: Isothiocyanates: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection at the site of an insect bite.
Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing itching and discomfort caused by an insect bite.
Alkaloids (specifically, baptifoline): Baptifoline has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help to reduce swelling, itching, and allergic reactions associated with insect bites.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to soothe and heal insect bite skin irritations.
Seeds: Utilized for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties to reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.
Leaves: Employed for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to provide relief and protection to the skin affected by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried roots and leaves of baptisia tinctoria from a trusted source in early spring.
Combine 250g of dried baptisia tinctoria with 500ml of oil in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids immediately.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 50ml of the infused liquid and heat the mixture in a double boiler until melted.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by insect bites, providing relief to affected skin.
Antiseptic: Chamomile's antiseptic properties help prevent infection and promote healing of bug bites, reducing the risk of bacterial or fungal infections.
Soothing agent: Chamomile's soothing properties calm irritated skin and reduce discomfort associated with bug bites, promoting a sense of relaxation and relief.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with insect bites. Additionally, chamomile's soothing and calming effects can help to ease the discomfort and promote healing of the affected area, making it a popular natural remedy for insect bite relief. This makes it a popular natural remedy for insect bite relief.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an antihistamine, reducing inflammation and itching caused by insect bites.
Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help calm irritated skin and reduce the discomfort associated with insect bites.
Alpha-bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling caused by insect bites, while also preventing infection.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make creams for insect bites because they contain anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm irritated skin.

Leaves: The leaves of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make creams for insect bites because they possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing swelling and promoting healing.

Seeds: The seeds of Matricaria chamomilla are used to make creams for insect bites because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and protect the skin from insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers that are free from pesticides and contaminants.
Infuse 250ml of carrier oil in a double boiler with 50g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for 2-3 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the solids from the liquid.
Mix 50g of beeswax with 50g of shea butter and 50ml of the infused matricaria chamomilla oil in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture for 10-15 minutes or until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency suitable for application.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.

Antihistamine properties: Nettle creams have natural antihistamine properties, which help neutralize histamines released during an allergic response to bug bites, reducing redness, itching, and hives.
Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in nettle cream help dry up fluid-filled bug bites, such as welts and blisters, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of infection.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica, also known as stinging nettle, creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream's active compounds, such as quercetin and caffeic acid, work to reduce redness and itching caused by insect venom. By reducing inflammation and alleviating itching, Urtica dioica creams provide quick relief from the discomfort and pain associated with insect bites. This natural remedy is often used as an alternative to traditional treatments, offering a soothing and effective solution for itchy insect bites.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (particularly Histamine-Releasing Factor): This constituent helps by reducing inflammation and alleviating itching caused by insect bites, as it mimics the action of histamine, a chemical released by the body in response to bites.

Triterpenes (particularly Urtic acid): Urtic acid, a triterpene present in Urtica dioica, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing redness and swelling associated with insect bites.

Phenolic acids (particularly Gallic acid): Gallic acid, a phenolic acid found in Urtica dioica, has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in soothing and calming the skin affected by insect bites.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of histamine and other anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce itching and swelling.
Roots: Utilized for their ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, thanks to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Employed for their similar properties to leaves, including histamine and anti-inflammatory compounds that provide relief from insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest urtica dioica leaves and stems in late spring or early summer when they are in their prime condition.
Chop 100g of urtica dioica leaves and stems into small pieces and dry them in a warm place for 2 weeks.
Combine the dried urtica dioica with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl and discard the solids, retaining 100g of the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help reduce swelling and redness caused by insect bites, providing quick relief from discomfort.
Antioxidant: Plantain's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals generated by insect venom, which can prevent further irritation and damage to the skin.

Soothing and moisturizing: The emollient properties of plantain cream soothe and calm irritated skin, while also providing long-lasting hydration and protection from environmental stressors that may exacerbate bug bite reactions.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantago major, also known as plantain, creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream is typically made from the extract of the plantain leaves, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to calm and reduce swelling caused by insect bites. The plantain's active compounds, such as aucubin and caffeic acid, help to reduce redness, itching, and pain, promoting a quick recovery from the bite. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to alleviate discomfort and itching caused by insect bites.
remedy_constituents: Apolipins: These flavonoid glycosides have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.
Apolavin: This flavonoid glycoside has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help soothe and calm the skin affected by insect bites, reducing redness and discomfort.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic acid has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infection and reduce inflammation caused by insect bites.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help soothe and calm insect bite reactions.
Seeds: The seeds are used because they contain mucilage, a natural gel-like substance that helps to reduce swelling and itching associated with insect bites.

Roots: The roots are used because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and the risk of infection caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried plantago major leaves in late summer when the seeds are mature.
Infuse 2 cups of distilled water with the dried plantago major leaves for 4-6 hours.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Blend the infused liquid with 1/4 cup of shea butter and the beeswax mixture until smooth.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, help reduce swelling and redness caused by insect bites, making them an effective ingredient in soothing bug bite relief.

Antimicrobial properties: Comfrey's antimicrobial agents, including triterpenoid saponins and phenolic acids, work to combat bacterial infections that can occur due to insect bites, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing.

Wound-healing properties: Allantoin, a key compound in comfrey, promotes collagen synthesis and tissue repair, helping to accelerate the healing process and reduce scarring associated with insect bites.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale, also known as comfrey, creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream's active compounds, such as allantoin and rosmarinic acid, work together to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with insect bites. Comfrey cream also possesses antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infection and promote healing of the affected area. By soothing and calming the skin, comfrey cream provides relief and promotes the body's natural recovery process from insect bites.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: It helps to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, which can alleviate the discomfort and swelling associated with insect bites.
Saponins: They have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe itchy skin, prevent infection, and reduce the risk of further irritation.
Flavonoids: They possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to calm the skin, reduce redness and swelling, and promote the healing of insect bite wounds.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of allantoin, which helps soothe and heal insect bites.
Roots: Roots are also used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing swelling and discomfort caused by insect bites.
Stems: Stems are sometimes used due to their content of saponins, which can help to reduce itching and irritation from insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of symphytum officinale roots and leaves in late summer or early autumn.
Dry the plant material at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours using a low-temperature oven.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the powder with 500 grams of emulsifying wax and 250 grams of coconut oil for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture to 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes using a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Witch Hazel's astringent properties help to reduce inflammation, itching, and redness caused by insect bites, by constricting blood vessels and drying up the affected area.

Antiseptic properties: The antiseptic properties of Witch Hazel cream help to prevent infection and promote wound healing by killing bacteria and other microorganisms that may be present on the skin after an insect bite.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Witch Hazel's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling, redness, and pain caused by insect bites, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing itchy bumps and welts.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana, also known as American witch hazel, creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The tannins present in witch hazel help to reduce the swelling and itching associated with insect bites. It also has antiseptic properties that help to prevent infection and promote healing. By applying witch hazel cream to the affected area, you can alleviate the discomfort and promote quick recovery from insect bites. This natural remedy is a popular choice for those seeking a gentle and effective solution.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling caused by insect bites.
Hamamelitannin: Hamamelitannin, a type of gallotannin, has potent antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help prevent infection and reduce itching and discomfort from insect bites.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): Flavonoids in Hamamelis virginiana have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation, itching, and pain caused by insect bites.
remedy_parts: Buds: Buds are used due to their high concentration of tannins, which provide anti-inflammatory and astringent properties to soothe insect bites.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce itching, redness, and swelling caused by insect bites.
Barks: Barks are used for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing inflammation and soothing irritated skin affected by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers on a sunny day in late summer.
Dry the plant material in a low-temperature oven at 30c for 2 hours or in a dehydrator.
Infuse 10 grams of dried hamamelis virginiana in 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 10 grams of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60c for 30 minutes then pour into jars to cool.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin, which can reduce swelling and redness caused by insect bites and stings.
Antibacterial property: Herbal licorice creams often contain antibacterial agents like tea tree oil or aloe vera, which can prevent infection and promote healing of the affected area.
Sothing and calming property: Licorice root has anti-irritant properties that can help soothe and calm irritated skin caused by insect bites, reducing itching and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compound glycyrrhizin in licorice root reduces redness, swelling, and itching caused by insect bites. It also has antiseptic properties that prevent infection and promote healing. By applying a topical cream containing licorice extract, individuals can find relief from the discomfort and pain associated with insect bites, allowing the skin to recover faster and minimizing the risk of scarring.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Flavonoids (Isoliquiritigenin and Liquiritigenin): These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with insect bites.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the discomfort and itching caused by insect bites.
Asteranes: These triterpenoid saponins have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce the risk of infection and promote healing of the skin after an insect bite.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhizin is extracted from the roots, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties to relieve itching and redness caused by insect bites.
Leaves: The leaves contain glycosides that help to reduce swelling and itching associated with insect bites.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in soothing and protecting the skin after an insect bite.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 25 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 500 milliliters of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Combine the infused oil with the beeswax and shea butter mixture and heat until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress caused by insect bites, promoting faster healing.
Saponins: The saponins present in dandelion creams have antimicrobial properties that help to combat bacterial infections often associated with insect bites, such as impetigo and cellulitis.
Triterpenoids: Dandelion triterpenoids, like taraxasterol, exhibit anti-pruritic (itch-relieving) effects, reducing the intense itching sensation caused by insect bites and promoting comfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The plant's natural compounds, such as taraxasterol and chicoric acid, have been found to reduce itching and swelling caused by insect bites. These properties make dandelion creams an effective remedy for soothing itchy skin and reducing the discomfort associated with insect bites, providing quick relief and promoting a smooth healing process. Its natural composition also makes it a gentle and non-irritating option.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: A triterpenoid that helps to reduce inflammation and itching associated with insect bites by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Taraxanthin: A carotenoid that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin affected by insect bites.
Alkaloids (including Taraxacum alkaloids): These compounds may help to reduce the histamine release in response to insect bites, thereby alleviating symptoms such as itching, redness, and swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their extracts are used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help alleviate itching and redness associated with insect bites.
Roots: The roots' extracts are used because of their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which aid in reducing swelling and preventing infection from insect bites.
Seeds: The seeds' extracts are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in soothing and protecting the skin from further damage caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom for maximum potency.
Combine the harvested taraxacum officinale with 200g of coconut oil in a stainless steel pot.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes to infuse the taraxacum officinale into the coconut oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a glass container and discard the solids immediately.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken before whipping it into a creamy texture with a hand mixer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herbal cream soothes the itching, redness, and swelling caused by insect bites, providing instant relief from discomfort. Its cooling effect also reduces heat and irritation, allowing the affected area to heal faster. Additionally, lemon verbena's antimicrobial properties prevent infection, making it an effective natural remedy for treating insect bites without harsh chemicals or artificial ingredients.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as citral and limonene, help reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.
Antiseptic properties: The antimicrobial agents present in lemon verbena cream, including a-pinene and camphor, work to prevent infection and promote healing of bite wounds.
Astringent properties: Lemon verbena's astringent compounds, like limonoid, help reduce redness, itching, and inflammation by constricting blood vessels and drying out the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon verbena have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and preventing infection from insect bites.
Seeds: Lemon verbena seeds contain fatty acids and antioxidants that help soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried verbena officinalis leaves and 10 grams of coconut oil in a glass jar.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler for 30 minutes until it reaches 65°c, stirring occasionally.
Add the dried verbena officinalis leaves to the heated oil and let it steep for 60 minutes, shaking the jar every 15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids, and store at room temperature for 24 hours.
Whip the infused oil with 10 grams of beeswax and 2 grams of shea butter until it thickens and emulsifies, then package in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. When applied topically, it reduces redness, swelling, and itching caused by bug bites. The cream's soothing effects calm the skin, providing fast relief from discomfort and irritation. Additionally, St John's Wort's natural antimicrobial properties help prevent infection and promote healing. As a result, applying herbal St John's Wort creams can bring quick and gentle relief to itchy, bite-prone areas, making them an excellent remedy for summer bug bites.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic property: St John's Wort cream has antiseptic properties, which help to prevent infection and reduce the risk of secondary bacterial infections from insect bites.
Anti-inflammatory property: St John's Wort cream has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling, redness, and itching caused by insect bites.
Antipruritic property: St John's Wort cream has antipruritic properties, which help to soothe and relieve itching caused by insect bites, providing rapid relief from the discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and itching caused by insect bites.
Barks: The barks contain a high amount of hyperforin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in soothing insect bite symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried hypericum perforatum flowers at a dosage of 20 grams for every 100 milliliters of cream base.
Infuse the dried hypericum perforatum flowers in 100 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes and then strain.
Combine the infused hypericum perforatum liquid with 50 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil at room temperature.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a consistency suitable for whipping into a creamy texture within 30 minutes.
Whip the mixture to incorporate air and achieve the desired cream consistency, taking approximately 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower creams helps with insect bites because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The extracts from cornflowers contain flavonoids and saponins, which work together to reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by bites. The cream also has a calming effect on the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, cornflower's antiseptic properties help to prevent infection and promote healing, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating the discomfort associated with insect bites.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Cornflower cream's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with insect bites, providing relief from discomfort.

Antihistamine property: The antihistamine property in cornflower cream counteracts the allergic reaction caused by insect bites, reducing histamine release and alleviating symptoms such as itching, hives, and inflammation.
Antioxidant property: Cornflower cream's antioxidant property helps soothe and calm the skin, protecting it from further irritation and damage caused by insect bite-induced oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Cornflower roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing and calming itchy skin caused by insect bites.
Barks: The barks of the cornflower plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centaurea cyanus flowers and leaves then dry them completely in shade or with low heat.
Combine 2 tablespoons dried flower and leaf mixture with 1 tablespoon coconut oil and 2 tablespoons beeswax in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low flame stirring constantly until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches a smooth consistency.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool slightly then add 10 drops lavender essential oil and 5 drops tea tree essential oil.
Pour the cooled mixture into small glass jars filling them to the top and store them in a cool dry place for up to 2 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream's active compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, work to reduce redness and swelling caused by bug bites. Additionally, the antimicrobial effects help prevent infection and promote healing. This natural remedy provides a soothing and calming relief from itchy and painful insect bites, making it an effective alternative to harsh chemical-based creams or ointments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Joe Pye Weed contains anti-inflammatory compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.

Antimicrobial activity: The cream's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause infection and exacerbate insect bite reactions, promoting healing and preventing further irritation.
Soothing and calming effects: Joe Pye Weed's essential oils have a soothing and calming effect on the skin, which helps to reduce redness, itching, and discomfort associated with insect bites.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and preventing infection from insect bites.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and vitamins, they help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and flowers in the early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or air circulation method for 24 hours.
Combine 10 grams of dried eupatorium perfoliatum with 20 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat until the beeswax melts, then allow it to cool and thicken for 30 minutes.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized jars and label accordingly, storing them at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with insect bites because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream soothes itchy skin, reducing redness and swelling caused by bug bites. Lemon balm's cooling effect also calms irritated areas, providing instant relief from discomfort. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help prevent infection and promote faster healing. As a result, applying lemon balm cream to insect bites can provide quick and effective relief from the discomfort and itchiness associated with these pesky bites.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine properties: Lemon balm cream's antihistamine properties help to reduce itching and inflammation caused by insect bites, allowing for faster healing and relief from discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream work to soothe and calm the skin, reducing swelling and redness associated with bug bites and stings.
Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help to prevent infection by killing bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can enter the wound through an insect bite or sting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, making them an effective ingredient in soothing and calming irritated skin caused by insect bites.
Leaves: Lemon balm leaves have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and redness associated with insect bites, allowing for faster healing and relief.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20g of dried melissa officinalis flowers and 100ml of sweet almond oil in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried herbs with 50ml of hot distilled water to create a decoction that steeps for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth into a separate container discarding solids and reserving liquid.
Mix the herbal liquid with 200ml of sweet almond oil in a double boiler heating gently over low heat for 30-40 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool before transferring to glass jars filled to within an inch of the top.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marsh dandelion creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and taraxin, work together to reduce swelling and itching caused by bug bites. Additionally, marsh dandelion's natural antioxidants help soothe the skin and promote healing. As a result, applying marsh dandelion cream to affected areas can provide rapid relief from discomfort and reduce the risk of infection. Its natural ingredients also make it an effective alternative to chemical-based creams and ointments.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marsh dandelion creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol, which helps reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.
Antihistamine properties: The cream's antihistamine compounds, such as sesquiterpenes, help alleviate allergic reactions to insect bites by blocking histamine release, reducing itching and redness.

Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help prevent infection by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause further complications from insect bites.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of taraxasterol, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in soothing and calming insect-bite reactions.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are employed for their ability to reduce itching, swelling, and redness associated with insect bites, thanks to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum palustre leaves measuring 50 grams in the early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content to 5%.
Blend the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a 100-mesh sieve to remove lumps.
Melt 200 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and mix with the powdered taraxacum palustre, adding 2% w/w of beeswax for thickening and stability.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and store in the refrigerator at 4°c to allow the ingredients to infuse for 14 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream's active ingredient, angelica archangelica, has been shown to reduce swelling, itching, and redness associated with insect bites. It also contains soothing compounds that calm the skin, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healing. Additionally, herbal angelica creams may help prevent infection by inhibiting bacterial growth, making them a natural and effective solution for relieving insect bite symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Angelica's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling, redness, and itching associated with insect bites, providing quick relief to affected areas.
Antibacterial: The antibacterial properties of angelica cream can combat the bacteria introduced by an insect bite, preventing infection and promoting healing of the skin.
Analgesic: Angelica's analgesic properties help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by insect bites, making it a natural remedy for soothing itchy and painful skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling caused by insect bites.
Barks: Containing bioactive compounds that help to reduce itching and inflammation, providing relief from itchy skin caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and dry them in shade for several days until they become brittle.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle or blender with 1 part by weight of angelica archangelica to 4 parts by weight of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.
Combine the ground angelica archangelica with beeswax in a double boiler and heat at low temperature for approximately 20 minutes until the mixture reaches a consistency of smooth cream.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth to remove any impurities and discard the solids retaining only the clear herbal infusion.
Fill sterilized glass jars with the prepared cream and store in refrigerator for up to one month or freeze for longer storage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for insect bites are topical remedies that utilize the healing properties of various plants to alleviate the discomfort and inflammation caused by insect stings or bites. The best herbal creams for insect bites include Calendula officinalis, Hypericum perforatum, Arnica montana, Echinacea purpurea, Aloe vera, Plantago major, Aloe barbadensis, Hamamelis virginiana, Symphytum officinale, and Melaleuca alternifolia. If left untreated, insect bites can lead to painful swelling, itching, and even anaphylaxis, significantly impacting a person's quality of life, causing sleep disturbances, and affecting daily activities.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to insect bites that are treated with herbal creams are skin irritations, rashes, and minor burns. Herbal creams can provide relief from symptoms such as itching, redness, and swelling. For example, creams containing aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, while creams with capsaicin or menthol can reduce pain and itching associated with minor burns and sunburns.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with insect bites is often a blend of Calendula, Aloe Vera, and Tea Tree. Calendula cream soothes and calms the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Aloe Vera cream provides long-lasting hydration and cooling relief, while Tea Tree oil offers antimicrobial properties to prevent infection. When combined, these creams can effectively alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with insect bites, promoting a quick and natural recovery.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by the Saudi Journal of Biological Sciences, herbal creams derived from medicinal plants can be effective in alleviating symptoms of insect bites due to their potential to reduce envenomation symptoms. The study reviewed the use of 92 medicinal plants, with 41% of them being leaves, which were preferred for therapeutic application, suggesting that certain plant extracts may be beneficial in soothing insect bites. This is particularly advantageous as herbal medicines are free from side effects, unlike chemical antidotes or antivenom therapy.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The active compounds in Calendula, such as triterpenoids and carotenoids, have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with insect bites. These compounds work by soothing the skin, reducing irritation, and promoting healing. By applying Calendula cream to the affected area, individuals can experience relief from the discomfort and pain caused by insect bites, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of infection.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help reduce inflammation and itching caused by insect bites by creating a protective barrier on the skin's surface.
Flavonoids: They have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help alleviate redness, swelling, and itching associated with insect bites.
Triterpenoids: They exhibit strong anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and heal the skin irritated by insect bites, while also preventing infection.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties help soothe and protect the skin from insect bites.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects in treating insect bites.
Seeds: The seeds are rich in unsaturated fatty acids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing after an insect bite.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 calendula officinalis flowers when in full bloom to ensure optimal potency and fragrance.
Infuse 2 cups of the harvested flowers in 4 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to extract properties.
Strain the infused liquid through cheesecloth into a clean bowl to separate the liquid from solids.
Combine 1/2 cup of the strained liquid with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully incorporated.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The cream's active ingredient, hyperforin, has been shown to reduce swelling, itching, and redness associated with insect bites. It also possesses antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infection from the bite. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects can provide relief from the discomfort and pain caused by the bite, promoting a faster healing process and reducing the risk of complications.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: It exhibits anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties, which help in reducing the itching and swelling caused by insect bites.
Quercetin: It is a flavonoid with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing the redness and itching associated with insect bites.
Naphthoquinone: It has antimicrobial properties, which help in preventing secondary infections that may occur due to insect bites.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe insect bites.
Flowers: They are used because they contain flavonoids and hyperforin, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in healing and reducing the discomfort of insect bites.
Stems: They are used due to their content of hyperforin and other bioactive compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve itching associated with insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 500 milliliters of vegetable oil for infusion.
Infuse the flowers in the oil for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the infused oil.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax and 150 grams of shea butter with the infused oil in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously over low heat until the beeswax and shea butter melt and the cream thickens.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream contains compounds that reduce swelling, redness, and itching caused by insect bites. It also has antiseptic properties that help prevent infection and promote healing. The cooling sensation of Arnica montana creams provides relief from the discomfort and pain associated with insect bites, making it a popular natural remedy for soothing itchy and irritated skin caused by insect bites. It accelerates the healing process, providing quick relief.
remedy_constituents: Helenalin: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce swelling and redness caused by insect bites.
Alantolactone: It has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate pain and itching associated with insect bites.
Caffeic acid: It has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the severity of the reaction to insect bites and promote healing.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in treating insect bites.
Leaves: Leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe and calm insect bite reactions.

Roots: Roots are a rich source of sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids, contributing to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and are often used in combination with flowers for enhanced efficacy.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh arnica montana flowers at peak bloom and dry them completely in a dark place for 2 weeks.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried arnica montana flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 1 tablespoon of arnica montana powder to the beeswax and coconut oil mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool and thicken for 30 minutes before applying to the skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with insect bites because they contain anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe and calm the affected area. The cream reduces itching, redness, and swelling by inhibiting the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for inflammation. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has natural antimicrobial properties that prevent infection and promote healing. By applying the cream to insect bites, individuals can experience relief from discomfort and speed up the recovery process, allowing the skin to return to its normal state.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation and swelling caused by insect bites, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
Caffeic Acid Derivatives (such as Cichoric Acid): These phenolic compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin affected by insect bites.

Alkylamides: These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help to reduce the severity of allergic reactions and itching associated with insect bites.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are used because they contain high concentrations of active compounds that provide anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used because they contain alkaloids that help to reduce itching and inflammation caused by insect bites.
Leaves: Leaves are used because they have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Infuse echinacea purpurea flowers in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil for 2 to 4 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to separate solids.
Combine the infused oil with a natural emollient such as beeswax and aloe vera gel in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax and emollient mixture in the double boiler while stirring occasionally for 10 to 15 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and thicken before transferring it to a storage container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with insect bites because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloe-emodin and aloin, which have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with insect bites. The cooling sensation and moisturizing properties of aloe vera creams can also help to alleviate itching and discomfort caused by bites. As a result, aloe vera creams can provide quick relief and promote the healing of insect bites.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: They help with insect bites by reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, thereby alleviating discomfort and pain caused by bites.
Saponins: They help with insect bites by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm the affected area.
Aloin: They help with insect bites by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling caused by bites.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain soothing compounds that help reduce inflammation and itching caused by insect bites.
Gel from Leaves: The gel extracted from aloe vera leaves has anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the skin and alleviate discomfort from insect bites.
Aloe Vera Juice from Leaves: Aloe vera juice, also extracted from the leaves, is used to create creams that help to reduce swelling and itching caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of aloe vera gel and mix it with 20 grams of vegetable glycerin in a clean bowl.
Combine 10 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a separate saucepan and melt at low heat.
Add the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture to the aloe vera and glycerin mixture and stir well.
Add 2 drops of vitamin e oil and 5 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir thoroughly.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Plantago major creams helps with insect bites because they contain anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties found in the plant's leaves. The cream reduces swelling, redness, and itching caused by insect stings. Plantago major, also known as plantain, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to soothe skin irritations. Its active compounds, such as aucubin and baicalein, help to calm the affected area, promoting a faster healing process and providing relief from the discomfort caused by insect bites.
remedy_constituents: Aucubin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with insect bites.
Plantamajoside: Exhibits anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe and calm irritated skin caused by insect bites.
Asperuloside: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to reduce inflammation and promote healing of skin affected by insect bites.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most used part due to their high content of allantoin, which helps to soothe and calm skin irritations caused by insect bites.
Seeds: Seeds are used for their astringent properties, which help to reduce inflammation and itching caused by insect bites.
Roots: Roots are used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to alleviate pain and prevent infection from insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves in the early morning when they are at their most potent for medicinal use.
Dry the plantago major leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to preserve their potency.
Infuse the dried plantago major leaves in a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove the plant material.
Mix the infused oil with a thickening agent such as beeswax and a preservative like vitamin e oil in a ratio of 1:1:0.1.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with insect bites because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloe-emodin and aloin, which have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with insect bites. These natural anti-inflammatory agents help to calm the skin, reducing itching and discomfort. By applying Aloe vera cream to the affected area, you can alleviate the symptoms of insect bites and promote a faster healing process, providing relief from the discomfort and pain caused by these bites.
remedy_constituents: Aloin: This anthraquinone compound helps by reducing inflammation and itching associated with insect bites, while also promoting wound healing.
Catechins: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm the skin after an insect bite.
Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone compound exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by insect bites.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves contain gel, which is rich in anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making it effective in reducing itching and swelling caused by insect bites.
Gel (from Leaves): The gel extracted from Aloe barbadensis leaves has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to calm and protect the affected skin from further irritation.

Aloe Vera Extract (from Leaves): Aloe vera extract, derived from the leaves, contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce redness, itching, and pain associated with insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 mature leaves of aloe barbadensis from the field with a sharp knife cutting.
Extract gel from the leaves by scooping it out with a spoon in 5 minutes.
Mix 100g of aloe gel with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler at 160°f for 10 minutes.
Add 50g of shea butter to the mixture and melt it at 180°f for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hamamelis virginiana creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The extract from the Witch Hazel plant has natural astringent and soothing qualities that help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching caused by insect bites. It also has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infection and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and promoting skin health, Hamamelis virginiana creams provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with insect bites, allowing the skin to recover quickly.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: They help to reduce inflammation and itching by contracting blood vessels and providing a protective barrier on the skin's surface.

Flavonoids (including Quercetin and Kaempferol): They have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce the allergic response and alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with insect bites.
Hamamelitannin: It has potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and protect the skin from infection, reducing the risk of complications from insect bites.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to soothe and calm irritated skin.
Barks: Utilized for their astringent and antiseptic properties to reduce swelling and prevent infection.
Stems: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to provide relief from itching and pain caused by insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh hamamelis virginiana leaves and flowers in the early morning when the dew is still present.
Combine the harvested plant material with 200ml of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 30 minutes to release the plant's bioactive compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil with the cooled herbal extract and whip until a smooth cream forms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams helps with insect bites because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The active compound, symphytum, in this herbal cream has been traditionally used to reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by insect bites. It calms irritated skin, reduces itching, and promotes healing. By applying Symphytum officinale cream to affected areas, individuals can find relief from the discomfort and swelling associated with insect bites, allowing the skin to recover more quickly. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective solution for insect bite relief.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: Helps to soothe and reduce inflammation of the skin caused by insect bites, promoting faster healing and relief.
Iridoid glycosides: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling associated with insect bites, and also helps to inhibit histamine release.
Saponins: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to calm the skin, prevent infection, and reduce the discomfort caused by insect bites.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in allantoin, which helps to soothe and heal skin irritations.
Roots: They contain allantoin, mucilage, and other compounds that reduce inflammation and promote skin regeneration.
Stems: They also contain allantoin, which helps to calm and protect the skin from further irritation.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried symphytum officinale roots in 1 liter of boiling water for 10 minutes then let cool.
Strain the infusion and mix 1 cup of the liquid with 1/2 cup of a carrier oil such as jojoba oil.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the mixture and heat it gently in a double boiler until the wax dissolves.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 120 degrees fahrenheit then whip until it thickens.
Pour the cream into a sterilized container and let it cool completely before sealing and labeling.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with insect bites because they contain the active ingredient tea tree oil, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. This oil helps to reduce swelling and itching associated with insect bites, while also preventing infection. The antimicrobial properties of tea tree oil also help to soothe and calm the skin, making it an effective and natural remedy for relieving the discomfort caused by insect bites. Regular application can provide quick relief and promote healing.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Help reduce inflammation and itching caused by insect bites by acting as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): Reduce pain and inflammation associated with insect bites by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals and acting as a local anesthetic.
Triptolide: Help alleviate itching and inflammation by inhibiting the production of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic reactions and itching sensations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high content of terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Buds: The buds are used because they contain a high concentration of the compound cineole, which has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks are used as they contain various compounds that have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm insect bites.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 300g of base cream containing 20g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter.
Combine 20g of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil with 80g of distilled water in a separate container.
Heat the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler until fully melted within 10 minutes.
Gradually add the essential oil mixture to the melted base cream and mix until well combined.
Pour the final mixture into containers and allow to cool and set within 30 minutes.
